title: Genesis: Creation and Covenant Beginnings
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of genesis: creation and covenant beginnings and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study genesis: creation and covenant beginnings and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What two realms does God create on the first day and second day in Genesis 1? Answer: light and darkness on day one, and the expanse separating the waters on day two.
segments.correct_answer: light and darkness on day one, and the expanse separating the waters on day two
acceptable_variants: day one is light and darkness, day two is sky and waters
light on day one, sky on day two
day one light day two firmament

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: Latin phrase meaning image of God; the biblical teaching that human beings are made in God's likeness and bear his representative authority over creation (Genesis 1:26-28)
The seventh day on which God rested from creation; established as a pattern of ceasing work in honor of God's completed work
